name: Stealth Archer
description:
Rarely-ambient Dungeon Synth / VGM, pulling in styles and influence from [gestures vaguely]. Ponder: meta-retrofuturist hauntological dumbassery.

Anti-NS, anti-AI, pro-LGBT, pro-P2P. Take what you must, pay for what you can.

Eternal hails to all fellow DIY musicians and artists of any medium.

Hail Texas Dungeon Synth!

name: Osees
description: Psych-punk psychic warrior, ear worm-farmer, and possessor of many stamped passport pages John Dwyer does not let up. His group Oh Sees (aka Thee Oh Sees, OCS, The Oh Sees, etc) have transmogrified to fit many a moment - from hushed druggy folk to groovy demonic pop chants to science fictional krautrock expanse and beyond - to suit his omnivorous whims.

name: Holy Wave
description: Holy Wave is an experimental dream-pop band originally from El Paso, Texas. After seeing a life-altering performance by My Bloody Valentine in 2008, the band moved to Austin to pursue their own vision of Texas psych, their sweet melodies dyed in carefully constructed noise.

name: American Sharks
description: American Sharks is a three-piece band hailing from Austin, TX. Their punk-metal hybrid sound blends pop hooks with blasting- yer-face riffs resulting in something akin to if Weezer went Stoner Rock.

name: Kind Keith
description: keyboardist, songwriter, fan of pokemon

name: Prehuman
description: Alternative grunge rock from ATX

name: BRUCE
description:
you're home alone in the shower; you're singing "all by myself" by celine dion at the top of your lungs while ice cold water prickles your skin into that of a chicken. from somewhere that seems far away, a stadium full of people throw flowers at your feet and chant your name. your name is BRUCE.

BRUCE is an art rock project. BRUCE got ready for sunday school all by herself.   less

name: Team Trust
description: Team Trust wants you to join the team. This three piece art rock band believes in curiosity, courage, and confidence. Formed in 2023, the group has been delivering melodic, dissonant, grooving, clunking, driving, sputtering songs to the tune of uniting people against the scourge of hyper-individualism. Team Trust is set to release their debut album this fall.

name: Égaux Sells
description:
Égaux
| (e•go) |
noun, plural,
1.equals

Vibrantly bleak pop experiment

name: Celestina Gravely
description: Alternative indie rock from Austin, TX

name: Mikky and the Doom
description: When Mikky steps onto the stage in her 9 inch heels, she gives the people what they want: a larger than life rockstar and thunderous vocals to boot. The members, a nebulous and ever-expanding roster of Austin’s best rockers, add an extra glitter of drama when they take the stage alongside her. Together, the band’s raw, eccentric performance and campy pageantry transport audiences back in time as Mikky & The Doom brings the glam back to rock. With catchy anthems, sleazy bass lines, and unmatched physicality, they know how to set fire to any crowd and leave an ear worm in your head. For those who are nostalgic for the golden era of rock ‘n roll but relish the spectacle of modern pop, Mikky & The Doom is the act to follow.

name: Swimwear Department
description: dance-punk about swimming pools and shopping malls

name: Tele Novella
description: Tele Novella is a project out of Lockhart, Texas--a small town lost in time---where their classic and sincere pop songwriting is slowly processed through a loner medieval-tonk machine and then captured on cassette 8-track. Their latest record, Poet's Tooth, steers the duo’s whimsical western-tinted balladry in fascinating new directions. It's out now on Kill Rock Stars.

name: Evan Autin
description: 🎸Funky Southern Blues Rocker

name: Sixteen Deluxe
description: Sixteen Deluxe is a psychedelic noise-pop band from Austin. Led by frontwoman Carrie Clark & guitarist/producer Chris "Frenchie" Smith, the band was active from 1994 to 2000, releasing 3 full-length albums, 3 7"'s, and 2 EP's on indie and major labels. They reunited in 2010, put out a 7" of new songs in 2015, and continue to play and record together only after consulting an arcane cosmic calendar.

name: The Midnight Papers
description: The Midnight Papers is a two-piece garage rock/protopunk band in Houston, Texas. Their debut album was chosen as one of Houston Chronicle's New Music Releases You Need to Hear in 2025.

name: Ari & the Artifacts
description: Introspective indie rock 😌 and pensive power pop 👀 from the heart (of Texas) 💯. Debut single “Getting Grown” out now!

name: Coatie Pop
description: coatie pop is a post punk trip hop grunge rave duo based in NYC

name: Wetsuit
description: brooklyn-based, water-based indie rock.

name: Meredith "Scottie" Welborn
description: Singer, Guitarist, Songstress. Alt-Everything.

name: Mitts
description: Mitts is a Texas-based songwriter, quietly writing songs under the stairs while the rest of the house sleeps. Most mornings start before dawn in a small closet-turned-studio, where songs come together, through an old Tascam into his DAW. Drawing from indie rock, Americana, and an overactive inner monologue, the music blends specificity with humor, offering a crooked grin to life’s heavier moments.

name: Kim Simpson
description: Kim Simpson is a singer-songwriter, guitarist, author and radio host who lives in Austin, Texas.

name: Nathan Felix & the Dirty Rotten Boys
Calico Sky
description:
Calico Sky is the electropop project of brothers Jonathan and Joel. Before diving deep into the synthwave vibes of '80s-inspired tech noir, the Texas twins collaborated with artists from all over the map—from Austin art-rock royalty …And You Will Know Us by the Trail of Dead to the electronic pioneer and producer Moby.

It was in the neon-lit alleys of Los Angeles where they honed their craft, playing in Hollywood’s storied dive bars while crystallizing the sounds that would become Calico Sky.

After years of working in L.A., and homesick for Texas, Jon and Joel moved back to Austin. Fueled by the city’s endless creative energy and love for its deep-rooted live music scene, the twins of Calico Sky invite listeners on a sonic journey where nostalgia meets futurism, and old-school melodies collide with a new wave of sound.

"Get offline and come to a show. We'll buy you a Lone Star."

name: Moving Panoramas
description: Moving Panoramas make boot gaze dream rock, led by songwriter, Leslie Sisson.

name: Elegant Tiger
description: Formerly known as the Austin, TX alt-rock act, Shapescenes, Elegant Tiger is an evolution in the sonic maximalism of Chase Anderson, Mark Son, and Justin Babineaux. The group has expanded to include Joe Miles on keys, guitar, and vocals.

name: JunkBunny
description: Combining hardcore hooks with a reverence for early 2000s pop-punk, JunkBunny’s sound is anthemically massive while still retaining emotional resonance. Compromised of Mac Johnson (Guitar/Vocals), Isaac Moore (Drums), and Dylan Dreiling (Bass), the Austin-based trio released their debut album Starting Over on May 3rd. Balancing gigs at gritty dive bars with amphitheaters alongside legendary acts like ZZ Top and Jon Bon Jovi, JunkBunny’s emergence has been as swift and visceral as the sounds they continue laboring over in an underground Austin Studio.

name: Multimagic
description: Multimagic is an American indie pop band led by songwriters & producers Coran Stetter and James Ruehlman. After a manic episode & hospitalization, Stetter began rebuilding his life through music. Ruehlman joined, & together they transformed their personal struggles and anti-capitalist perspective into Manic Daze, their acclaimed debut album. New single “Heart” arrives May 16,

name: Spirit Ghost
description: Never gonna make it.

name: The Owling
description: the OWLiNG is a heavy, melodic duo from Hollywood blending doom, cinematic riffs, and ’90s alt-metal into a dark, riff-driven sound.

name: Carriers
description:
Led by Curt Kiser, Carriers is Cincinnati’s best-kept secret, blending heartland rock with dreamy, introspective Americana. With a devoted local following and support from indie heavyweights, Carriers is ready to take the national stage.

“Hallucinatory heartland rock…dreamy, Dylan-esque.”

name: The Lost Millions
description: The Lost Millions, from Austin, Texas, consists of Matt Westfield on vocals, Heath McBurnett on bass, and Scott Bettersworth on drums. Heavy grooves, fast attack, and swimming melodies, the trio delivers powerful rock n' roll. With their unique sound and electrifying stage presence, The Lost Millions have quickly become one of the most sought-after bands in the Austin music scene.

name: Gun Cat
description: (atx post-rock with weird tendencies)

name: Happy Phantom
description:
Happy Phantom is a Post Punk, alternative Rock, band formed by front man, singer/song writer, and multi instrumentalist Jackie Legos.

Vocals/Guitars: Jackie Legos,
Keyboards/Synth: Austin Hayse
Bass Guitars: Dave Ainsworth

Additional guest musicians:
Percussion: Colby Sias
Bass Guitar: Phillip Sozansky
Guitars: Brendan Ladan
Drums: Zack Bigalow

name: Weird Wolves
description:
A blend of darkwave, electro-rock, and synth-pop

Formed in 2018 by Raphael Colantonio, acclaimed video game creator (Arkane Studios) known for designing immersive video games like Prey and Dishonored.

Weird Wolves has evolved into a full band

Current band members include Celestina Gravely and Zeph Rexx. Past band members include Ava Gore and Ben Post.

name: Death of the Last Beautiful Jaguar
description: Death of the Last Beautiful Jaguar is the solo project of Houston based artist Anthony Torres

name: Jet Cemetary
description: Austin, TX, duo TaSzlin Trébuchet (Fuck Money) and Lars Wolfshield (Wolfshield Records) come together in Jet Cemetery, an Electronic project exploring ethereal textures and dystopian soundscapes with probing, poignant vocals.

name: The Violet Mindfield
description: The Violet Mindfield is a four piece, 60's inspired, American, Garage/Surf/Psych/Bedroom/Acid Folk/Lo-fi/among other things/Rock'n Roll band, based in and around the Inland Empire of Southern California. The group consists of John Duran (lead vocals/guitar), Harry Parr (drums/backing vocals), Sean Mcbreen (bass) and Clifton Weaver (organ/harmonica/percussion/guitar).

name: Hikes
description: Hikes is a band of friends from Austin, Tx. Inspired by nature, and driven to meld their love of heavy, technical rock with folk

name: Club Coma
description: C L U B C O M A, New Austin based experimental group comprised of Scott Martin, Aaron Perez and Geoff Earle pulls together an exciting, genre-bending sound that lives somewhere between Rock n Roll, Experimental Pop, and mid-aughts French touch.

name: Daphne Tunes
description: sunday morning tunes from the blue heart of tx.

name: Paper Sister
description: Dream pop alt post shoegaze band from Austin, Texas.

name: Living Set
description: Living Set is an Austin-based band blending post- punk tension, new wave melody, and indie rock intimacy. Made up of seasoned musicians—Quin Galavis (The Dead Space, solo), Lex Trevino, Annecy Liddell (Knife in the Water), and Matt Hammer (The Strange Boys, Black Joe Lewis, OBN III’s)—the band delivers songs that are reflective, rhythm-forward, and emotionally grounded.

name: The Lovely Sparrows
description:
Jones’s lyrics vividly animate spectral rodeo clowns, accidental tourists, and the vanished frontier.

Their third LP moves through liminal spaces and objects out of time, building songs from secret codes and inside jokes.

In the quiet after it ends, I Still Picture You Running seems to keep moving—music chasing its own echo across the wide field of memory, haunted and humane.

name: Blanky
description: Badger Hunt (NY) = Miles (TX) & Jamie (SC)

name: Dutch Kills
description: The Dutch Kills are a femme rock band based in Brooklyn. The group is comprised of guitarist/vocalist Julia Von Dutch, guitarist Alec Berry, bassist Maxine McCormick, and drummer Heather Jensen.

name: The Opera
description: The Opera is a 6 piece art-punk band based in Austin, TX.

name: Broken Colours
description:
Hailing from High Wycombe, Broken Colours are a four-piece indie outfit who draw on the funked-up, rocky sensibilities to delivery truly electrifying performances. Taking cues from the likes of Jamiroquai and Arctic Monkeys, the band burst onto the scene with their acclaimed self-titled EP in 2016.

Touring the UK, Denmark and Italy, the band have shared the stage with the likes of The Blockheads, Jack Bugg, The Zutons and The Subways, reinforcing their must-see dynamic live shows. Looking forward to 2025, the band look to reassert themselves as a creative force to be reckoned with, with new music on the horizon.

name: Livermore
description:
Formed in March 2025, Livermore brings to the stage guitarists Marley Kezerian and Carter Stanley along with mandolinist Jacob Mink. After meeting in the jam scene (shout out to CTBA) around Austin, the trio decided to start making music together. Looking to reinforce the group's sound, the trio brought Joslyn Boyer into the band to hold down the low end on bass. 

Informed by traditions and standards, but not afraid to branch out into other styles, you'll never guess what we've got coming next.

name: Clement Hall
description: Clement Hall is an indie/alternative rock band in Lubbock, TX. The group was formed by frontman Luke Hamaker & bassist Aidan Haydu in their college dorm. Soon after, they welcomed members Quinn Edgar, Gustavo Oliveira, and Tyler Cush. The band has sold out venues and clubs around their college town and continue to release airy indie rock integrated with pop and disco characteristics

name: THEMM!
description: Twenty three (23) years of Austin's fun rock slop-punk and free drink tickets. Formerly known as The Midgetmen, now known as THEMM!

name: This Is Lorelei
description: this is lorelei and i am nate and my other band is called water from your eyes

name: Ashaine White
description:
Just when you thought every genre mash-up had been done, Ivor Novello Rising Star Nominee Ashaine White arrives with a brand new hybrid: grunge-soul.

pinned as bandcamp’s best of soul (june 2023) her new EP “Ash” is out now!

name: Sailor Poon
description: Doomy stoogey post-punk from Austin, TX

name: Taraneh
description: currently based in nyc, eternally located in the middle of nowhere

name: Twink Death
description: gay trans furry emo

name: Wonderdya
description: my name is anna catherine and i love music. i hope you like it here. thank you for listening :)

name: TV's Daniel
description: Originating in Austin, Texas, TV’s Daniel formed in 2017, igniting a new punk ethos era that the city so deeply yearned for. Lead singer, Daniel Fried is no stranger to music, playing in bands such as Bad Sports, Radioactivity, and VIDEO. After decades of collaborating with other people, Daniel decided he wanted to create his own band, his own sound, with his own principles.

name: Kevin Morby
description: Kevin Morby writes (and records, and imagines) at an almost incomparable clip, and his most recent album, This Is A Photograph, studies life, time and mortality through myriad lenses. It’s a dynamic, buoyant record on big, heavy themes, so it only makes sense that Morby found he wasn’t quite done with it on its completion. More Photographs (A Continuum) finds new nooks, corners and vantage points.

name: Dylan Earl
description:
I’m from where I woke up this morning. I write the songs that come to mind and struggle with the ones that don’t. I find humor in misconceptions and solace in laughter, love in the street and fire in the eyes of the marginalized. I prefer facts over alternate facts.
I’m here to shoot you straight, although sometimes I fire from the hip.
I’ll always love you.
~Dearl

name: Emily Nenni
description: A singer-songwriter from the Bay Area, Nenni released her debut album, "Hell of a Woman," in 2018. Her musical inspiration ranges from girl groups of the 1960s to outlaw country of the 1970s. She has honed her honky tonk skills in double wide trailers and on Sunday nights, clubs across the south, as well as played for ranchers and wranglers in Wyoming, Montana, and Colorado. TRR are her boys.

name: Jonny Fritz
description:
Call the Hotline
1(844) DAD-FARTD

name: Plume Girl
description: Plume Girl is the alt-experimental solo project of Hindustani classical singer and composer, Sowmya Somanath

name: Hexpartner
description: Neoclassical darkwave in Austin, TX

name: Lili Lonelyhearts
description: Cursed Folk Musician

name: Georgia Britt
description: an artist that stumbled into the music scene. art to me is a way to convey your true self and it can also emulate the world in which we exist. merch is now available! buy something and support me dude!

name: Jenny Parrott
description: Independent Musician living in Austin TX. Solo Debut Album "When I Come Down". Second album "Fire I Saw" Nov 21'. "Pregnancy Choir" EP coming October 28th 2022

name: Al Lover
description:
Neu!-Age Spiritualized Repetition

Creating a broken abstraction of trip hop, Al Lover incorporates elements of psych, shoegaze, proto-electronica, krautrock & dub to draw out elements from the past and connect them to the future.

name: Analog Dog
description: Analog Dog makes genre fluid music in San Francisco.

name: Shelby Spector
description: Shelby Specter is a genre-bending artist from Texas, blending '70s metal, '80s new wave, and '90s alt-punk into a sound both savage and soulful. Her voice—somewhere between Elvis Presley’s velvet and Chris Cornell’s flame—defies expectation. First rising during the COVID lockdown, she later fronted Digger of Dirt, a fierce DIY band known for relentless shows and a month-long tour. Now solo again, she returns with Aether, a debut album full of grit, grief, and ghostlight. Onstage, Shelby pairs occult glamour with punk fury, turning every performance into a ritual. Audiences may judge the boots, the eyeliner, the smirk, but her songs peel back every illusion. She writes for the wild ones, sings for the forgotten, and fights for love, justice, and misfit kin.

name: Keaton Schiller
description:
Keaton Schiller is a New Orleans-based singer and songwriter. 

With her warm yet gritty melodies and strikingly intimate lyrics, Schiller’s songs live in a world of contradiction. They carry conflicting emotions, multiple perspectives, and confront both dreams and reality. Schiller’s atmospheric music and candid voice create an indie rock sound that is both fresh and familiar. Her songs are filled with hooks, loaded with feeling, and begin at the moment where things fall apart. 

schiller has planned and performed in multiple cross-country tours, most recently selling out New York’s Union Pool and D.c.’s beloved rhizome, becoming one of the most exciting rising voices in indie rock.

name: The Noir Dalis
description: As the name suggests, The Noir Dalis sound consists of beautiful, floral sonic soundscapes with an overall dark, ominous tone. Beginning as a personal project by Carlos Bojorquez in 2018, the Noir Dalis have evolved past their garage/psych influences into a stylistic collision of all its parts made possible by the collective minds of Carlos, Josenowsky and Tommy Graf

name: 39th Gate of Hell
description: A female-fronted Post-Punk alternative garage rock band combines punk attitude with dance grooves. Holding a foot in Sheffield and Austin, 39th Gate of Hell,  is a multinational trio based around Turkish-born spoken word artist,  Ayse Balkose and guitarist/singer,  Ash Gray from Austin, TX. The group switches easily from whimsical and fun to seriously poetic subjects for a heavy groove-led style.

name: Municipal Bats
description: Noise rock trio from Houston, TX. Raw dissonance, crushing riffs & primal rhythms. Inspired by Whores., The Jesus Lizard & Bauhaus. Chaos as art.

name: Big Violet
description: High Desert Americana Rock

name: Born Twins
description: The latest musical incarnation of songwriters Singer Mayberry and David Lujan (Red Leaves, Bitter Birds). They’ve been in groups together since the early 2000’s, but Born Twins is their most recent endeavor, with Mayberry taking the lead on guitar and vocals”, Lujan on Drums and Bass Organ, and the addition of third member Gwyneth Lujan on keys and synth.

name: Kids That Fly
description:
Kids That Fly formed in the fall of 2018, while attending the University of Connecticut. It wasn’t long before they fell into a steady rhythm of playing shows in and around Connecticut, while simultaneously commuting in and out of New York City to record and perform in both Manhattan and Brooklyn. Having all grown up in Connecticut, the crew has had a lot of influence from other Connecticut musicians such as Rivers Cuomo of Weezer. Each member has played music in the indie/alt rock spheres, even dabbled in jazz, but their broad collective musical taste includes The Black Keys, The 1975, The Killers, and Catfish and the Bottlemen — keeping them tied to a more alternative rock sound.

Praised by outlets like Atwood Magazine for their “captivating indie rock anthems” and Music Connection Magazine for their “polished, powerfully fun pop-rock,” the band has also been lauded by The Aqua

name: Ethan Azarian
description: Hopelessly original, refreshingly out of touch, Ethan Azarian, raconteur from Vermont has become an entirely respectable Austin singer/songwriter/painter. While some songwriters turn inward for subject material, Azarian wholeheartedly embraces well-trod universal themes of love, death, and rebirth without the crutches of irony or melodrama - Greg Beets, Austin Chronicle

name: Ramblin' Deano
description: Dean Schlabowske's career as a songwriter, singer and guitarist spans over 30 years, most notably as a co-founder of Chicago's Waco Brothers. Dean is also involved in many side projects such as Dollar Store, collaborations with Austin, Texas musicians in Ice Cold Singles and a solo acoustic project under the moniker "Ramblin' Deano".

name: Jon Dee Graham & William Harries Graham
Broken Gold
description:
Broken Gold is a 4 piece indie punk rock band from Austin TX.
Their new album "Wild Eyes" is out now!
Ian M-Vox/gtr
Ben Lance-gtr
Bobby D-Bass
Sam Rich-Drums
